title: Humanity Has Altered an Asteroid’s Orbit Around the Sun
summary: A NASA spacecraft into a small asteroid in 2022 moved its orbit around the Sun, according to a study that presents the “first-ever measurement of human-caused change in the heliocentric orbit of a celestial body.”
reason: A scientific milestone about asteroid deflection, this story has no meaningful connection to the framework's core concerns about enclosure, commoning, or capture mechanisms.

title: He saw an abandoned trailer. Then, he uncovered a surveillance network on California's border
summary: Southern California residents are noticing new license plate readers that appear to be operated by the Border Patrol. Some have had confusing encounters with agents.
reason: The story concretely shows surveillance infrastructure being routed through everyday public space — border enforcement expanding its capture architecture into civilian life without public deliberation.
angle: Use 'routing architecture' to examine how Border Patrol is extending its detection grid beyond the border itself, and 'alibi structure' to ask what legal and political fictions make this expansion seem unremarkable.

title: California colleges spend millions on faulty AI systems: 'The chatbot is outdated’
summary: Community colleges are spending millions on AI-powered chatbots that students say often give inaccurate answers. Many might see upgrades this year.
reason: This illustrates epistemic incompetence of centralization: institutions are replacing distributed human advising knowledge with centralized AI systems that systematically destroy the local, relational intelligence they depend on.
angle: Use the 'epistemic incompetence of centralization' tool to show how routing student questions through vendor-controlled AI destroys the distributed knowledge that made community college advising functional in the first place.
